title: selaginella procera
intro: Selaginella procera, also known as the "King's Fern" or "Spiny Club Moss," is a type of vascular plant that belongs to the Selaginellaceae family and is native to tropical regions. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been found to aid in wound healing and reduce fever. The health benefits of Selaginella procera include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce oxidative stress, and exhibit antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. In herbal preparations, Selaginella procera is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also applied topically to the skin to treat various skin conditions and infections.
